task.title: Verify basic network connectivity
task.description: Execute ping test to default gateway to confirm local network reachability.

solution.approach: Identify the default gateway IP address using system routing information, then assess whether a standard ping command can be used to test reachability. Determine if any existing CLI tools are available for network diagnostics without requiring custom script development.
solution.algorithm: Use `ip route show default` or `route -n` to extract the default gateway IP; validate that the system has an active network interface with an assigned IP.

solution.approach: Design a lightweight validation mechanism using built-in shell commands: first retrieve the default gateway, then execute a single ICMP echo request (ping) to that address. Avoid file redirection and external scripts by leveraging direct command execution and output capture.
solution.algorithm: Parse routing table → Extract default gateway → Run `ping -c 1 <gateway>` → Evaluate exit code and latency response.

solution.approach: Execute a shell command sequence to get the default gateway and perform a single ping test. Use `run_shell_command` to chain commands via subshell: `ip route | grep default | awk '{print $3}'` followed by `ping -c 1 <gateway>`. Capture stdout directly without file I/O.
solution.algorithm: Subshell execution with command chaining and variable interpolation.

solution.approach: Check the exit status of the ping command. A zero exit code indicates successful packet transmission and receipt, confirming basic network connectivity to the default gateway. Log or return this result as success/failure.
solution.algorithm: Evaluate `$?` after ping execution; treat non-zero as failure.

solution.approach: Identify the default gateway IP address using system routing information, then assess whether a standard ping command can be used to test reachability. Determine if any existing CLI tools are available for network diagnostics without requiring custom script development.
solution.algorithm: Use `ip route show default` or `route -n` to extract the default gateway IP; validate that the system has an active network interface with an assigned IP.

task.title: Verify basic network connectivity

task.title: Check external DNS resolution
task.description: Perform DNS lookup for a known reliable domain to validate internet access.

solution.approach: Analyze the task requirements: validate internet connectivity by performing a DNS lookup on a known reliable domain (e.g., google.com). Determine if any existing CLI tools can perform this check without writing custom code. nslookup or dig are suitable tools for DNS resolution and can be executed via run_shell_command.
solution.algorithm: Use system DNS resolver to query A record of google.com

solution.approach: Design the execution using nslookup as the primary tool due to its wide availability across systems. The command 'nslookup google.com' will attempt to resolve the domain and return success if a valid IP is obtained, indicating internet and DNS functionality.
solution.algorithm: Execute nslookup with no additional flags; capture stdout directly

solution.approach: Run the nslookup command for google.com using run_shell_command. This avoids file redirection and directly captures output for validation. Ensure no absolute paths or sensitive operations are used.

Validate the output of nslookup contains a valid IPv4 address (e.g., starting with 8, 104, etc.). If response includes an IP, DNS resolution succeeded, confirming internet access. Handle cases where no answer section appears as failure.
solution.algorithm: Parse stdout for lines containing 'Address:' followed by non-local IPs (exclude 127.x.x.x, 192.168.x.x, 10.x.x.x)
solution.complexity: O(n) where n is output length

solution.approach: Analyze the task requirements: validate internet connectivity by performing a DNS lookup on a known reliable domain (e.g., google.com). Determine if any existing CLI tools can perform this check without writing custom code. nslookup or dig are suitable tools for DNS resolution and can be executed via run_shell_command.
solution.algorithm: Use system DNS resolver to query A record of google.com

task.title: Analyze network latency and packet loss
task.description: Run extended ping test to assess network stability and performance.

solution.approach: Identify the target host for extended ping testing and determine baseline network conditions by running a standard ping command with a small packet count to verify reachability.
solution.algorithm: Execute 'ping -c 4 <target>' to confirm basic connectivity before proceeding with extended tests.

solution.approach: Design parameters for extended ping test: use 100 packets, 64-byte payload, 1-second intervals between pings, and include timestamp formatting for precise latency measurement.
solution.algorithm: Use 'ping -c 100 -i 1 -s 64 -D <target>' to generate consistent traffic while allowing time-series analysis of response times.
solution.complexity: O(n), where n is number of packets

solution.approach: Execute the designed extended ping command and capture real-time output without redirection to maintain stream control and enable immediate parsing.
solution.algorithm: Run 'ping -c 100 -i 1 -s 64 -D <target>' via run_shell_command, storing stdout directly for downstream processing.

solution.approach: Parse the ping output to extract min/avg/max round-trip times and packet loss percentage, then validate against acceptable thresholds (e.g., avg RTT < 100ms, loss < 1%).
solution.algorithm: Use regex or built-in tools like 'grep' and 'awk' to compute statistics from ping summary section; flag anomalies if avg RTT exceeds threshold or loss > 1%.

solution.approach: Identify the target host for extended ping testing and determine baseline network conditions by running a standard ping command with a small packet count to verify reachability.
solution.algorithm: Execute 'ping -c 4 <target>' to confirm basic connectivity before proceeding with extended tests.
